one_time_description: Province of BC Reconciliation & Crisis Support funding received and deferred - $5,000,000
economic_notes: Nation has investments in fishing (Ehattesaht Fishing Corporation, Hayu Fishing Ltd., Hayu Fishing Limited Partnership), forestry (Aat'uu Forestry Limited Partnership), hydro (Barr Creek Hydro Ltd., Zeballos Lake Hydro Limited Partnership), consulting (Strategic Natural Resource Consultants Inc.), and real estate (Bastion Holdings Ltd., 0881643 BC Ltd.) enterprises. GBEs reported combined revenues of $6,318,301 and net loss of $1,735,895 in 2023.
notes: Audit is qualified because not all affiliated entities were audited, affecting the Investments in First Nation Affiliated Entities balance and GBE income. Nation has 2 consolidated subsidiaries (0754140 BC Ltd. and Aat'uu Forestry Ltd.) and 9 GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Large $5,000,000 Province of BC Reconciliation & Crisis Support funding received but fully deferred at year-end, causing a significant increase in deferred revenue from $534,002 to $5,445,473. Bad debt expense of $1,931,668 is unusually large and relates to write-off of Ehattesaht Fishing Corporation receivable. ISC funding not separately identified - NTC (Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council) appears to be the primary government transfer conduit. Total government transfers estimated as NTC ($2,588,751) + CMHC ($8,775) + Province of BC ($1,424,032) + First Peoples Heritage ($227,428) = $4,248,986. Wages and benefits per segment disclosure total $771,377 vs $1,007,341 per schedule of expenses by object - used object schedule figure for salaries total.
